title: Nephrodium heterocarpon
taxonomy: Nephrodium heterocarpon is classified under the division Pteridophyta, which comprises vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Further classification places it within the class Polypodiopsida, a group of ferns characterized by their reproductive structures. The order Polypodiales is also part of its classification, encompassing various families of ferns with similar characteristics. Nephrodium heterocarpon belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, which includes several species of ferns with similar traits. Its classification also includes the genus Nephrodium, which is now often considered synonymous with Dryopteris.

intro: Nephrodium heterocarpon, also known as Asplenium heterocarpon, is a species of fern belonging to the family Aspleniaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects.
